61: AdamW.__doc__ = (
62:     r"""Implements AdamW algorithm, where weight decay does not accumulate in the momentum nor variance.
63: 
64:     .. math::
65:        \begin{aligned}
66:             &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
67:             &\textbf{input}      : \gamma \text{(lr)}, \: \beta_1, \beta_2
68:                 \text{(betas)}, \: \theta_0 \text{(params)}, \: f(\theta) \text{(objective)},
69:                 \: \epsilon \text{ (epsilon)}                                                    \\
70:             &\hspace{13mm}      \lambda \text{(weight decay)},  \: \textit{amsgrad},
71:                 \: \textit{maximize}                                                             \\
72:             &\textbf{initialize} : m_0 \leftarrow 0 \text{ (first moment)}, v_0 \leftarrow 0
73:                 \text{ ( second moment)}, \: v_0^{max}\leftarrow 0                        \\[-1.ex]
74:             &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
75:             &\textbf{for} \: t=1 \: \textbf{to} \: \ldots \: \textbf{do}                         \\
76: 
77:             &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{maximize}:                                       \\
78:             &\hspace{10mm}g_t           \leftarrow   -\nabla_{\theta} f_t (\theta_{t-1})         \\
79:             &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{else}                                                           \\
80:             &\hspace{10mm}g_t           \leftarrow   \nabla_{\theta} f_t (\theta_{t-1})          \\
81:             &\hspace{5mm} \theta_t \leftarrow \theta_{t-1} - \gamma \lambda \theta_{t-1}         \\
82:             &\hspace{5mm}m_t           \leftarrow   \beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \beta_1) g_t          \\
83:             &\hspace{5mm}v_t           \leftarrow   \beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\beta_2) g^2_t          \\
84:             &\hspace{5mm}\widehat{m_t} \leftarrow   m_t/\big(1-\beta_1^t \big)                   \\
85:             &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: amsgrad                                                  \\
86:             &\hspace{10mm} v_t^{max} \leftarrow \mathrm{max}(v_{t-1}^{max},v_t)                  \\
87:             &\hspace{10mm}\widehat{v_t} \leftarrow v_t^{max}/\big(1-\beta_2^t \big)              \\
88:             &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{else}                                                           \\
89:             &\hspace{10mm}\widehat{v_t} \leftarrow   v_t/\big(1-\beta_2^t \big)                  \\
90:             &\hspace{5mm}\theta_t \leftarrow \theta_t - \gamma \widehat{m_t}/
91:                 \big(\sqrt{\widehat{v_t}} + \epsilon \big)                                       \\
92:             &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\[-1.ex]
93:             &\bf{return} \:  \theta_t                                                     \\[-1.ex]
94:             &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\[-1.ex]
95:        \end{aligned}
96: 
97:     For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization`_.
98:     """
99:     + rf"""
100:     Args:
101:         {_params_doc}
102:         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3). A tensor LR
103:             is not yet supported for all our implementations. Please use a float
104:             LR if you are not also specifying fused=True or capturable=True.
105:         betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing
106:             running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))
107:         eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve
108:             numerical stability (default: 1e-8)
109:         weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay coefficient (default: 1e-2)
110:         amsgrad (bool, optional): whether to use the AMSGrad variant of this
111:             algorithm from the paper `On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond`_
112:             (default: False)
113:         {_maximize_doc}
114:         {_foreach_doc}
115:         {_capturable_doc}
116:         {_differentiable_doc}
117:         {_fused_doc}
118:     .. Note::
119:         A prototype implementation of Adam and AdamW for MPS supports `torch.float32` and `torch.float16`.
120:     .. _Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization:
121:         https://arxiv.org/abs/1711.05101
122:     .. _On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond:
123:         https://openreview.net/forum?id=ryQu7f-RZ
124: 
125:     """
126: )
127:

129: # @_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported logic occurs in the decorator that's applied to F.adam
130: def adamw(
131:     params: list[Tensor],
132:     grads: list[Tensor],
133:     exp_avgs: list[Tensor],
134:     exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],
135:     max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],
136:     state_steps: list[Tensor],
137:     # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627
138:     # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim
139:     foreach: Optional[bool] = None,
140:     capturable: bool = False,
141:     differentiable: bool = False,
142:     fused: Optional[bool] = None,
143:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,
144:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,
145:     has_complex: bool = False,
146:     *,
147:     amsgrad: bool,
148:     beta1: float,
149:     beta2: float,
150:     lr: Union[float, Tensor],
151:     weight_decay: float,
152:     eps: float,
153:     maximize: bool,
154: ):
155:     r"""Functional API that performs AdamW algorithm computation.
156: 
157:     See :class:`~torch.optim.AdamW` for details.
158:     """
159:     adam(
160:         params,
161:         grads,
162:         exp_avgs,
163:         exp_avg_sqs,
164:         max_exp_avg_sqs,

24: def sparse_adam(
25:     params: list[Tensor],
26:     grads: list[Tensor],
27:     exp_avgs: list[Tensor],
28:     exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],
29:     state_steps: list[int],
30:     *,
31:     eps: float,
32:     beta1: float,
33:     beta2: float,
34:     lr: float,
35:     maximize: bool,
36: ):
37:     r"""Functional API that performs Sparse Adam algorithm computation.
38: 
39:     See :class:`~torch.optim.SparseAdam` for details.
40:     """
41:     for i, param in enumerate(params):
42:         grad = grads[i]
43:         grad = grad if not maximize else -grad
44:         grad = grad.coalesce()  # the update is non-linear so indices must be unique
45:         grad_indices = grad._indices()
46:         grad_values = grad._values()
47:         if grad_values.numel() == 0:
48:             # Skip update for empty grad
49:             continue
50:         size = grad.size()
51: 
52:         exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]
53:         exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]
54:         step = state_steps[i]
55: 
56:         def make_sparse(values):
57:             constructor = grad.new
58:             if grad_indices.dim() == 0 or values.dim() == 0:
59:                 return constructor().resize_as_(grad)
60:             return constructor(grad_indices, values, size)
61: 
62:         # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient
63:         #      old <- b * old + (1 - b) * new
64:         # <==> old += (1 - b) * (new - old)
65:         old_exp_avg_values = exp_avg.sparse_mask(grad)._values()
66:         exp_avg_update_values = grad_values.sub(old_exp_avg_values).mul_(1 - beta1)
67:         exp_avg.add_(make_sparse(exp_avg_update_values))
68:         old_exp_avg_sq_values = exp_avg_sq.sparse_mask(grad)._values()
69:         exp_avg_sq_update_values = (
70:             grad_values.pow(2).sub_(old_exp_avg_sq_values).mul_(1 - beta2)
71:         )
72:         exp_avg_sq.add_(make_sparse(exp_avg_sq_update_values))
73: 
74:         # Dense addition again is intended, avoiding another sparse_mask
75:         numer = exp_avg_update_values.add_(old_exp_avg_values)
76:         exp_avg_sq_update_values.add_(old_exp_avg_sq_values)
77:         denom = exp_avg_sq_update_values.sqrt_().add_(eps)
78:         del exp_avg_update_values, exp_avg_sq_update_values
79: 
80:         bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step
81:         bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step
82:         step_size = lr * math.sqrt(bias_correction2) / bias_correction1
83: 
84:         param.add_(make_sparse(-step_size * numer.div_(denom)))

318:     differentiable: bool,
319:     capturable: bool,
320:     has_complex: bool,
321: ):
322:     assert not differentiable, "_foreach ops don't support autograd"
323: 
324:     if len(params) == 0:
325:         return
326: 
327:     # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]
328:     if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:
329:         capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(
330:             supports_xla=False
331:         )
332:         assert all(
333:             p.device.type == step.device.type
334:             and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices
335:             for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)
336:         ), (
337:             f"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}."
338:         )
339: 
340:     lr = _to_scalar(lr)
341: 
342:     grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
343:         [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]
344:     )
345:     for (
346:         grouped_params_,
347:         grouped_grads_,
348:         grouped_exp_avgs_,
349:         grouped_exp_infs_,
350:         grouped_state_steps_,
351:     ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():
352:         grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)
353:         grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)
354:         grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)
355:         grouped_exp_infs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_infs_)
356:         grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)
357: 
358:         if has_complex:
359:             _view_as_real(
360:                 grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs
361:             )
362: 
363:         if maximize:
364:             grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]
365: 
366:         # Update steps
367:         # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over
368:         # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just
369:         # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.
370:         if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:
371:             torch._foreach_add_(
372:                 grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device="cpu"), alpha=1.0
373:             )
374:         else:
375:             torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)
376: 
377:         if weight_decay != 0:
378:             if maximize:
379:                 # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize
380:                 torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)
381:             else:
382:                 grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]
383:                     grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay
384:                 )
385: 
386:         # Update biased first moment estimate.
387:         torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)
388: 
389:         # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.
390:         torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_infs, beta2)
391: 
392:         # in this case, we need to introduce a copy of the grads
393:         # since one has not been introduced previously
394:         if not maximize and weight_decay == 0:
395:             grouped_grads = torch._foreach_abs(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]
396:         else:
397:             torch._foreach_abs_(grouped_grads)
398: 
399:         torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, eps)
400:         torch._foreach_maximum_(grouped_exp_infs, grouped_grads)
401: 
402:         bias_corrections: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]
403:         if capturable:
404:             bias_corrections = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, grouped_state_steps)
405:             # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg
406:             torch._foreach_sub_(bias_corrections, 1)
407:             torch._foreach_div_(bias_corrections, lr)
408: 
409:             denom = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_exp_infs, bias_corrections)
410:             torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, denom)
411:         else:
412:             bias_corrections = [
413:                 1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in grouped_state_steps
414:             ]
415:             step_size = [(_get_value(lr) / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_corrections]
416:             torch._foreach_addcdiv_(
417:                 grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs, step_size
418:             )
419:

arguments.content:
# PyTorch Foreach Optimizer Refactoring

## Task
Extract repeated `_multi_tensor_*` foreach optimization patterns into shared `torch/optim/_foreach.py`

## Key Findings

### Files with _multi_tensor functions
- sgd.py: `_multi_tensor_sgd`
- adam.py: `_multi_tensor_adam`
- adamw.py: (uses adam's implementation)
- asgd.py: `_multi_tensor_asgd`
- rprop.py: `_multi_tensor_rprop`
- radam.py: `_multi_tensor_radam`
- nadam.py: `_multi_tensor_nadam`
- adamax.py: `_multi_tensor_adamax`
- adagrad.py: `_multi_tensor_adagrad`
- rmsprop.py: `_multi_tensor_rmsprop`
- adadelta.py: `_multi_tensor_adadelta`
- _adafactor.py: `_multi_tensor_adafactor`

### Common Patterns to Extract
1. **Grouping by device/dtype**: `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype()`
2. **Complex parameter handling**: `_view_as_real()` and `_view_as_complex()`
3. **Maximize handling**: `torch._foreach_neg()` for gradients
4. **Step updates**: `torch._foreach_add_(state_steps, 1)`
5. **Weight decay**: Similar logic across all
6. **Assertions**: Early return if params empty, assert not differentiable for foreach
7. **Capturable checks**: Device compatibility checks for capturable mode
8. **Scalar conversion**: `_to_scalar(lr)` and `_get_value(step)`

### Implementation Plan
1. Create `torch/optim/_foreach.py` with helper functions
2. Update sgd.py, adam.py, adamax.py to use shared utilities
3. Maintain backward compatibility - keep public APIs unchanged

arguments.content:
# mypy: allow-untyped-defs
r"""Shared utilities for foreach optimizer implementations."""

from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, Union

import torch
from torch import Tensor

from .optimizer import (
    _get_capturable_supported_devices,
    _get_value,
    _stack_if_compiling,
    _to_scalar,
    _view_as_real,
    DeviceDict,
    DeviceDtypeDict,
    Optimizer,
)


__all__ = [
    "_foreach_optimizer_step",
    "_setup_foreach_group",
    "_apply_foreach_maximize",
    "_apply_foreach_weight_decay",
    "_update_foreach_step",
    "_foreach_addcdiv_with_step_size",
]


def _setup_foreach_group(
    grouped_tensors_items: tuple[tuple[list[Tensor], ...], Any],
    has_complex: bool,
    view_fn_list: Optional[list[Callable[[list[Tensor]], None]]] = None,
) -> tuple[tuple[list[Tensor], ...], Any]:
    """Setup foreach group by handling complex parameters.

    Args:
        grouped_tensors_items: Tuple from grouped_tensors.values()
        has_complex: Whether any parameter is complex
        view_fn_list: Optional list of view functions to apply to handle complex params

    Returns:
        Updated grouped_tensors_items with complex parameters converted to real view
    """
    if has_complex and view_fn_list is not None:
        tensors, indices = grouped_tensors_items
        for view_fn in view_fn_list:
            view_fn(tensors)
    return grouped_tensors_items


def _apply_foreach_maximize(
    grouped_grads: list[Tensor],
    maximize: bool,
) -> list[Tensor]:
    """Apply maximize by negating gradients if needed.

    Args:
        grouped_grads: List of gradient tensors
        maximize: Whether to maximize (negate gradients)

    Returns:
        Modified gradients (either in-place modified or new list if maximize=True)
    """
    if maximize:
        grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]
    return grouped_grads


def _apply_foreach_weight_decay(
    grouped_grads: list[Tensor],
    grouped_params: list[Tensor],
    weight_decay: float,
    maximize: bool,
) -> list[Tensor]:
    """Apply weight decay to gradients using foreach operations.

    Args:
        grouped_grads: List of gradient tensors
        grouped_params: List of parameter tensors
        weight_decay: Weight decay coefficient
        maximize: Whether maximize was applied (affects whether we can reuse memory)

    Returns:
        Modified gradients
    """
    if weight_decay != 0:
        # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) if maximize was applied
        if maximize:
            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)
        else:
            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]
                grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay
            )
    return grouped_grads


def _update_foreach_step(
    grouped_state_steps: list[Tensor],
) -> None:
    """Update state steps using foreach operations.

    Args:
        grouped_state_steps: List of step tensors
    """
    # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop
    # calling t.add(1) over and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over
    # again, which is slower than if we just wrapped it once now.
    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:
        torch._foreach_add_(
            grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device="cpu"), alpha=1.0
        )
    else:
        torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)


def _foreach_addcdiv_with_step_size(
    params: list[Tensor],
    numerator: list[Tensor],
    denominator: list[Tensor],
    step_size: Union[float, list[float], Tensor, list[Tensor]],
) -> None:
    """Apply addcdiv operation with step size scaling.

    Args:
        params: Parameter tensors to update
        numerator: Numerator tensors
        denominator: Denominator tensors
        step_size: Scalar or list of scalars/tensors
    """
    torch._foreach_addcdiv_(params, numerator, denominator, step_size)


def _foreach_optimizer_step(
    params: list[Tensor],
    grads: list[Tensor],
    state_list: Optional[list[dict[str, Tensor]]] = None,
    step_fn: Optional[Callable[..., None]] = None,
    **kwargs: Any,
) -> None:
    """Common foreach optimizer step with device/dtype grouping.

    This function provides a wrapper for foreach optimization steps that:
    1. Groups tensors by device and dtype
    2. Handles complex parameters
    3. Applies common operations (maximize, weight decay, step updates)
    4. Calls optimizer-specific step function

    Args:
        params: List of parameters
        grads: List of gradients
        state_list: Optional list of state dicts
        step_fn: Optimizer-specific step function to call for each group
        **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to step_fn
    """
    if len(params) == 0:
        return

    # Early validation
    differentiable = kwargs.get("differentiable", False)
    assert not differentiable, "_foreach ops don't support autograd"

    # Group tensors by device and dtype
    tensor_lists = [params, grads]
    if state_list is not None:
        tensor_lists.append(state_list)

    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
        tensor_lists,  # type: ignore[list-item]
        with_indices=kwargs.get("with_indices", False),
    )

    for group_data, indices in grouped_tensors.values():
        # Extract tensors for this group
        group_params = cast(list[Tensor], group_data[0])
        group_grads = cast(list[Tensor], group_data[1])
        group_state = None
        if state_list is not None:
            group_state = cast(list[dict[str, Tensor]], group_data[2])

        # Call optimizer-specific step function
        if step_fn is not None:
            step_fn(
                group_params,
                group_grads,
                group_state,
                **kwargs,
            )


def _foreach_group_tensors(
    tensor_lists: list[list[Tensor]],
    with_indices: bool = False,
) -> dict[tuple[torch.device, torch.dtype], tuple[tuple[list[Tensor], ...], Optional[list[int]]]]:
    """Group tensors by device and dtype.

    Args:
        tensor_lists: Lists of tensors to group
        with_indices: Whether to return indices

    Returns:
        Dictionary mapping (device, dtype) to tensor groups
    """
    return Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
        tensor_lists,  # type: ignore[list-item]
        with_indices=with_indices,
    )


def _check_capturable_devices(
    params: list[Tensor],
    state_steps: list[Tensor],
    supports_xla: bool = True,
) -> None:
    """Check if parameters are on supported devices for capturable mode.

    Args:
        params: Parameter tensors
        state_steps: State step tensors
        supports_xla: Whether XLA is supported

    Raises:
        AssertionError: If devices are not supported for capturable mode
    """
    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling():
        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(
            supports_xla=supports_xla
        )
        assert all(
            p.device.type == step.device.type
            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices
            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)
        ), (
            f"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}."
        )


def _convert_tensor_lr_to_dict(
    lr: Optional[Union[float, Tensor]],
    device: Optional[torch.device] = None,
    device_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = None,
) -> Optional[DeviceDict]:
    """Convert tensor lr to a device dictionary.

    Args:
        lr: Learning rate (float or Tensor)
        device: Target device
        device_dict: Existing device dictionary

    Returns:
        Updated device dictionary or None if lr is not a Tensor
    """
    if isinstance(lr, Tensor):
        if device_dict is None:
            device_dict = {lr.device: lr}
        elif device is not None and device not in device_dict:
            device_dict[device] = lr.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)
    return device_dict

def _foreach_optimizer_step(
    params: list[Tensor],

)

]


def _setup_foreach_group(
    grouped_tensors_items: tuple[tuple[list[Tensor], ...], Any],
    has_complex: bool,
    view_fn_list: Optional[list[Callable[[list[Tensor]], None]]] = None,
) -> tuple[tuple[list[Tensor], ...], Any]:
    """Setup foreach group by handling complex parameters.

    Args:
        grouped_tensors_items: Tuple from grouped_tensors.values()
        has_complex: Whether any parameter is complex
        view_fn_list: Optional list of view functions to apply to handle complex params

    Returns:
        Updated grouped_tensors_items with complex parameters converted to real view
    """
    if has_complex and view_fn_list is not None:
        tensors, indices = grouped_tensors_items
        for view_fn in view_fn_list:
            view_fn(tensors)
    return grouped_tensors_items


def _apply_foreach_maximize(
    grouped_grads: list[Tensor],
    maximize: bool,
) -> list[Tensor]:
    """Apply maximize by negating gradients if needed.

    Args:
        grouped_grads: List of gradient tensors
        maximize: Whether to maximize (negate gradients)

    Returns:
        Modified gradients (either in-place modified or new list if maximize=True)
    """
    if maximize:
        grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]
    return grouped_grads


def _apply_foreach_weight_decay(
    grouped_grads: list[Tensor],
    grouped_params: list[Tensor],
    weight_decay: float,
    maximize: bool,
) -> list[Tensor]:
    """Apply weight decay to gradients using foreach operations.

    Args:
        grouped_grads: List of gradient tensors
        grouped_params: List of parameter tensors

    if weight_decay != 0:
        # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) if maximize was applied
        if maximize:
            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)
        else:
            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]
                grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay
            )
    return grouped_grads

    grouped_state_steps: list[Tensor],

    # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop

            )


def _foreach_group_tensors(
    tensor_lists: list[list[Tensor]],
    with_indices: bool = False,
) -> dict[tuple[torch.device, torch.dtype], tuple[tuple[list[Tensor], ...], Optional[list[int]]]]:
    """Group tensors by device and dtype.

    Args:
        tensor_lists: Lists of tensors to group
        with_indices: Whether to return indices

    Returns:
        Dictionary mapping (device, dtype) to tensor groups
    """
    return Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
        tensor_lists,  # type: ignore[list-item]
        with_indices=with_indices,
    )


def _check_capturable_devices(
    params: list[Tensor],
    state_steps: list[Tensor],
    supports_xla: bool = True,
) -> None:
    """Check if parameters are on supported devices for capturable mode.

    Args:
        params: Parameter tensors
        state_steps: State step tensors
        supports_xla: Whether XLA is supported

    Raises:
        AssertionError: If devices are not supported for capturable mode
    """
    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling():
        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(
            supports_xla=supports_xla
        )
        assert all(
            p.device.type == step.device.type
            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices
            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)
        ), (
            f"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}."
        )


def _convert_tensor_lr_to_dict(
    lr: Optional[Union[float, Tensor]],
    device: Optional[torch.device] = None,
    device_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = None,
) -> Optional[DeviceDict]:
    """Convert tensor lr to a device dictionary.

    Args:
        lr: Learning rate (float or Tensor)
        device: Target device
        device_dict: Existing device dictionary

    Returns:
        Updated device dictionary or None if lr is not a Tensor
    """
    if isinstance(lr, Tensor):
        if device_dict is None:
            device_dict = {lr.device: lr}
        elif device is not None and device not in device_dict:
            device_dict[device] = lr.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)
    return device_dict

163:             &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
164:             &\textbf{for} \: t=1 \: \textbf{to} \: \ldots \: \textbf{do}                         \\
165:             &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{maximize}:                                       \\

173:             &\hspace{15mm} \textbf{b}_t \leftarrow \mu \textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\tau) g_t           \\
174:             &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{else}                                                          \\
175:             &\hspace{15mm} \textbf{b}_t \leftarrow g_t                                           \\
176:             &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{nesterov}                                       \\
177:             &\hspace{15mm} g_t \leftarrow g_{t} + \mu \textbf{b}_t                               \\
178:             &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{else}                                                   \\[-1.ex]

208:         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()
209:         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()

212:     __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf

214:     .. note::
215:         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from
216:         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.
217: 
218:         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as
219: 
220:         .. math::
221:             \begin{aligned}
222:                 v_{t+1} & = \mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\
223:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \text{lr} * v_{t+1},
224:             \end{aligned}
225: 
226:         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\mu` denote the
227:         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.
228: 
229:         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and
230:         other frameworks which employ an update of the form
231: 
232:         .. math::

235:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.
236:             \end{aligned}

238:         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.
239:

241:         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other
242:         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect
243:         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled
244:         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.

269:     r"""Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.

273:     # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change
274:     # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach
275:     # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl
276:     # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.

308:         weight_decay=weight_decay,

320: def _single_tensor_sgd(

322:     grads: list[Tensor],
323:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
324:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
325:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
326:     *,
327:     weight_decay: float,
328:     momentum: float,
329:     lr: float,
330:     dampening: float,
331:     nesterov: bool,
332:     maximize: bool,
333:     has_sparse_grad: bool,
334: ):

337:     if not torch.jit.is_scripting():
338:         lr = _to_scalar(lr)
339: 
340:     for i, param in enumerate(params):
341:         grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]
342: 
343:         if weight_decay != 0:
344:             # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules
345:             if isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):
346:                 if weight_decay.requires_grad:
347:                     # usually this is the differentiable path, which is why the param.clone() is needed
348:                     grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)
349:                 else:
350:                     grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)
351:             else:
352:                 grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)

354:         if momentum != 0:

363:             if nesterov:

366:                 grad = buf
367: 
368:         # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules

378: def _multi_tensor_sgd(
379:     params: list[Tensor],

398:     lr = _to_scalar(lr)

402:         with_indices=True,
403:     )
404:     for (
405:         device_params_,
406:         device_grads_,
407:         device_momentum_buffer_list,
408:     ), indices in grouped_tensors.values():
409:         device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)
410:         device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)
411: 
412:         device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(
413:             grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads
414:         )
415: 
416:         if maximize:
417:             device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]

419:         if weight_decay != 0:

421:             if maximize:
422:                 torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)
423:             else:
424:                 device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]
425:                     device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay
426:                 )
427: 
428:         if momentum != 0:
429:             bufs: list[Tensor] = []
430: 
431:             all_states_with_momentum_buffer = True
432:             for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):
433:                 if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:
434:                     all_states_with_momentum_buffer = False
435:                     break
436:                 else:
437:                     bufs.append(cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i]))
438: 
439:             if all_states_with_momentum_buffer:
440:                 torch._foreach_mul_(bufs, momentum)
441:                 torch._foreach_add_(bufs, device_grads, alpha=1 - dampening)
442:             else:
443:                 bufs = []
444:                 for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):
445:                     if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:

448:                         ] = device_grads[i].detach().clone()
449:                     else:
450:                         buf = cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i])
451:                         buf.mul_(momentum).add_(device_grads[i], alpha=1 - dampening)
452: 
453:                     bufs.append(buf)
454: 
455:             if nesterov:
456:                 torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, bufs, alpha=momentum)
457:             else:
458:                 device_grads = bufs
459: 
460:         if not device_has_sparse_grad:
461:             # handle internal item() call if lr is a tensor
462:             if isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor) and torch.compiler.is_compiling():
463:                 grads_x_lr = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, -lr)
464:                 torch._foreach_add_(device_params, grads_x_lr)
465:             else:
466:                 torch._foreach_add_(device_params, device_grads, alpha=-lr)
467:         else:
468:             # foreach APIs don't support sparse
469:             for i in range(len(device_params)):
470:                 device_params[i].add_(device_grads[i], alpha=-lr)
471: 
472: 
473: def _fused_sgd(
474:     params: list[Tensor],
475:     grads: list[Tensor],
476:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
477:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
478:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
479:     *,
480:     weight_decay: float,
481:     momentum: float,
482:     lr: float,
483:     dampening: float,
484:     nesterov: bool,
485:     maximize: bool,
486:     has_sparse_grad: bool,
487: ) -> None:
488:     if not params:
489:         return
490:     if has_sparse_grad:
491:         raise RuntimeError("`_fused_sgd` does not support sparse gradients")
492:     grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict = (
493:         {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}
494:     )
495:     found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (
496:         {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}
497:     )
498: 
499:     no_momentum_buffer = momentum == 0
500:     is_first_step = (
501:         all(t is None for t in momentum_buffer_list) and not no_momentum_buffer

515:         device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)

527:             if no_momentum_buffer
528:             else cast(list[Tensor], device_momentum_buffer_list),
529:             weight_decay=weight_decay,
530:             momentum=momentum,
531:             lr=lr,

533:             nesterov=nesterov,

# mypy: allow-untyped-defs
r"""Implementation for Stochastic Gradient Descent optimizer."""

from typing import cast, Optional, Union

import torch
from torch import Tensor

from ._foreach import (
    _apply_foreach_maximize,
    _apply_foreach_weight_decay,
)
from .optimizer import (
    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,
    _device_dtype_check_for_fused,
    _differentiable_doc,
    _foreach_doc,
    _fused_doc,
    _maximize_doc,
    _params_doc,
    _to_scalar,
    _use_grad_for_differentiable,
    DeviceDict,
    Optimizer,
    ParamsT,
)


__all__ = ["SGD", "sgd"]


class SGD(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101
    def __init__(
        self,
        params: ParamsT,
        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,
        momentum: float = 0,
        dampening: float = 0,
        weight_decay: Union[float, Tensor] = 0,
        nesterov: bool = False,
        *,
        maximize: bool = False,
        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,
        differentiable: bool = False,
        fused: Optional[bool] = None,
    ):  # noqa: D107
        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:
            raise ValueError("Tensor lr must be 1-element")
        if lr < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid learning rate: {lr}")
        if momentum < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid momentum value: {momentum}")
        if weight_decay < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}")

        defaults = {
            "lr": lr,
            "momentum": momentum,
            "dampening": dampening,
            "weight_decay": weight_decay,
            "nesterov": nesterov,
            "maximize": maximize,
            "foreach": foreach,
            "differentiable": differentiable,
            "fused": fused,
        }
        if nesterov and (momentum <= 0 or dampening != 0):
            raise ValueError("Nesterov momentum requires a momentum and zero dampening")
        super().__init__(params, defaults)

        if fused:
            self._step_supports_amp_scaling = True
            self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = True
            if differentiable:
                raise RuntimeError("`fused` does not support `differentiable`")
            if foreach:
                raise RuntimeError("`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.")

    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105
        super().__setstate__(state)
        for group in self.param_groups:
            group.setdefault("nesterov", False)
            group.setdefault("maximize", False)
            group.setdefault("foreach", None)
            group.setdefault("differentiable", False)
            group.setdefault("fused", False)

    def _init_group(self, group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list):
        has_sparse_grad = False

        for p in group["params"]:
            if p.grad is not None:
                if group["fused"] and getattr(
                    self, "_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused", True
                ):
                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p)
                    self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = False
                params.append(p)
                grads.append(p.grad)
                if p.grad.is_sparse:
                    has_sparse_grad = True

                if group["momentum"] != 0:
                    state = self.state[p]
                    momentum_buffer_list.append(state.get("momentum_buffer"))

        return has_sparse_grad

    @_use_grad_for_differentiable
    def step(self, closure=None):
        """Perform a single optimization step.

        Args:
            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model
                and returns the loss.
        """
        loss = None
        if closure is not None:
            with torch.enable_grad():
                loss = closure()

        for group in self.param_groups:
            params: list[Tensor] = []
            grads: list[Tensor] = []
            momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]] = []

            has_sparse_grad = self._init_group(
                group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list
            )

            sgd(
                params,
                grads,
                momentum_buffer_list,
                weight_decay=group["weight_decay"],
                momentum=group["momentum"],
                lr=group["lr"],
                dampening=group["dampening"],
                nesterov=group["nesterov"],
                maximize=group["maximize"],
                has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,
                foreach=group["foreach"],
                fused=group["fused"],
                grad_scale=getattr(self, "grad_scale", None),
                found_inf=getattr(self, "found_inf", None),
            )

            if group["momentum"] != 0:
                # update momentum_buffers in state
                for p, momentum_buffer in zip(params, momentum_buffer_list):
                    state = self.state[p]
                    state["momentum_buffer"] = momentum_buffer

        return loss


SGD.__doc__ = (
    r"""Implements stochastic gradient descent (optionally with momentum).

    .. math::
       \begin{aligned}
            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
            &\textbf{input}      : \gamma \text{ (lr)}, \: \theta_0 \text{ (params)}, \: f(\theta)
                \text{ (objective)}, \: \lambda \text{ (weight decay)},                          \\
            &\hspace{13mm} \:\mu \text{ (momentum)}, \:\tau \text{ (dampening)},
            \:\textit{ nesterov,}\:\textit{ maximize}                                     \\[-1.ex]
            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
            &\textbf{for} \: t=1 \: \textbf{to} \: \ldots \: \textbf{do}                         \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{maximize}:                                       \\
            &\hspace{10mm}g_t           \leftarrow   -\nabla_{\theta} f_t (\theta_{t-1})         \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{else}                                                           \\
            &\hspace{10mm}g_t           \leftarrow   \nabla_{\theta} f_t (\theta_{t-1})          \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \lambda \neq 0                                           \\
            &\hspace{10mm} g_t \leftarrow g_t + \lambda  \theta_{t-1}                            \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \mu \neq 0                                               \\
            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{if} \: t > 1                                                   \\
            &\hspace{15mm} \textbf{b}_t \leftarrow \mu \textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\tau) g_t           \\
            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{else}                                                          \\
            &\hspace{15mm} \textbf{b}_t \leftarrow g_t                                           \\
            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{nesterov}                                       \\
            &\hspace{15mm} g_t \leftarrow g_{t} + \mu \textbf{b}_t                               \\
            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{else}                                                   \\[-1.ex]
            &\hspace{15mm} g_t  \leftarrow  \textbf{b}_t                                         \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\theta_t \leftarrow \theta_{t-1} - \gamma g_t                    \\[-1.ex]
            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\[-1.ex]
            &\bf{return} \:  \theta_t                                                     \\[-1.ex]
            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\[-1.ex]
       \end{aligned}

     Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from
     `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.
     """
    + rf"""
     Args:
         {_params_doc}
         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)
         momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)
         dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)
         weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)
         nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable
             when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)
         {_maximize_doc}
         {_foreach_doc}
         {_differentiable_doc}
         {_fused_doc}
     """
    + r"""

     Example:
         >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP
         >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)
         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()
         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()
         >>> optimizer.step()

     __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf

     .. note::
         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from
         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.

         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as

         .. math::
             \begin{aligned}
                 v_{t+1} & = \mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\
                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \text{lr} * v_{t+1},
             \end{aligned}

         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\mu` denote the
         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.

         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and
         other frameworks which employ an update of the form

         .. math::
             \begin{aligned}
                 v_{t+1} & = \mu * v_{t} + \text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\
                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.
             \end{aligned}

         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.

         Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the
         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other
         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect
         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled
         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.

     """
)


def sgd(
    params: list[Tensor],
    d_p_list: list[Tensor],
    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627
    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim
    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,
    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,
    fused: Optional[bool] = None,
    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,
    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,
    *,
    weight_decay: float,
    momentum: float,
    lr: float,
    dampening: float,
    nesterov: bool,
    maximize: bool,
):
    r"""Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.

    See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.
    """
    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change
    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach
    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl
    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.
    if foreach is None and fused is None:
        # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?
        # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting
        if not torch.jit.is_scripting():
            fused, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(
                params, differentiable=False, use_fused=False
            )
        else:
            foreach = False
            fused = False
    if foreach is None:
        foreach = False
    if fused is None:
        fused = False

    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():
        raise RuntimeError("torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers")
    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():
        raise RuntimeError("torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers")

    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():
        func = _multi_tensor_sgd
    elif fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():
        func = _fused_sgd
    else:
        func = _single_tensor_sgd

    func(
        params,
        d_p_list,
        momentum_buffer_list,
        weight_decay=weight_decay,
        momentum=momentum,
        lr=lr,
        dampening=dampening,
        nesterov=nesterov,
        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,
        maximize=maximize,
        grad_scale=grad_scale,
        found_inf=found_inf,
    )


def _single_tensor_sgd(
    params: list[Tensor],
    grads: list[Tensor],
    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
    *,
    weight_decay: float,
    momentum: float,
    lr: float,
    dampening: float,
    nesterov: bool,
    maximize: bool,
    has_sparse_grad: bool,
):
    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None

    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():
        lr = _to_scalar(lr)

    for i, param in enumerate(params):
        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]

        if weight_decay != 0:
            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules
            if isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):
                if weight_decay.requires_grad:
                    # usually this is the differentiable path, which is why the param.clone() is needed
                    grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)
                else:
                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)
            else:
                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)

        if momentum != 0:
            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]

            if buf is None:
                buf = grad.detach().clone()
                momentum_buffer_list[i] = buf
            else:
                buf.mul_(momentum).add_(grad, alpha=1 - dampening)

            if nesterov:
                grad = grad.add(buf, alpha=momentum)
            else:
                grad = buf

        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules
        if isinstance(lr, Tensor):
            if lr.requires_grad:
                param.addcmul_(grad, lr, value=-1)
            else:
                param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)
        else:
            param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)


def _multi_tensor_sgd(
    params: list[Tensor],
    grads: list[Tensor],
    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
    *,
    weight_decay: float,
    momentum: float,
    lr: float,
    dampening: float,
    nesterov: bool,
    maximize: bool,
    has_sparse_grad: bool,
):
    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None

    if len(params) == 0:
        return

    lr = _to_scalar(lr)

    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  # type: ignore[list-item]
        with_indices=True,
    )
    for (
        device_params_,
        device_grads_,
        device_momentum_buffer_list,
    ), indices in grouped_tensors.values():
        device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)
        device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)

        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(
            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads
        )

        # Use shared utility for maximize
        device_grads = _apply_foreach_maximize(device_grads, maximize)

        # Use shared utility for weight decay
        device_grads = _apply_foreach_weight_decay(
            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize
        )

        if momentum != 0:
            bufs: list[Tensor] = []

            all_states_with_momentum_buffer = True
            for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):
                if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:
                    all_states_with_momentum_buffer = False
                    break
                else:
                    bufs.append(cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i]))

            if all_states_with_momentum_buffer:
                torch._foreach_mul_(bufs, momentum)
                torch._foreach_add_(bufs, device_grads, alpha=1 - dampening)
            else:
                bufs = []
                for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):
                    if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:
                        buf = device_momentum_buffer_list[i] = momentum_buffer_list[
                            indices[i]
                        ] = device_grads[i].detach().clone()
                    else:
                        buf = cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i])
                        buf.mul_(momentum).add_(device_grads[i], alpha=1 - dampening)

                    bufs.append(buf)

            if nesterov:
                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, bufs, alpha=momentum)
            else:
                device_grads = bufs

        if not device_has_sparse_grad:
            # handle internal item() call if lr is a tensor
            if isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor) and torch.compiler.is_compiling():
                grads_x_lr = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, -lr)
                torch._foreach_add_(device_params, grads_x_lr)
            else:
                torch._foreach_add_(device_params, device_grads, alpha=-lr)
        else:
            # foreach APIs don't support sparse
            for i in range(len(device_params)):
                device_params[i].add_(device_grads[i], alpha=-lr)


def _fused_sgd(
    params: list[Tensor],
    grads: list[Tensor],
    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
    *,
    weight_decay: float,
    momentum: float,
    lr: float,
    dampening: float,
    nesterov: bool,
    maximize: bool,
    has_sparse_grad: bool,
) -> None:
    if not params:
        return
    if has_sparse_grad:
        raise RuntimeError("`_fused_sgd` does not support sparse gradients")
    grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict = (
        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}
    )
    found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (
        {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}
    )

    no_momentum_buffer = momentum == 0
    is_first_step = (
        all(t is None for t in momentum_buffer_list) and not no_momentum_buffer
    )
    if is_first_step:
        for i, g in enumerate(grads):
            momentum_buffer_list[i] = torch.empty_like(g)
    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  # type: ignore[list-item]
        with_indices=False,
    )
    for (device, _), (
        (device_params_, device_grads_, device_momentum_buffer_list),
        _,
    ) in grouped_tensors.items():
        device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)
        device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)
        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None
        if grad_scale is not None:
            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict.setdefault(
                device, grad_scale.to(device)
            )
        if found_inf_dict is not None and found_inf is not None:
            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict.setdefault(device, found_inf.to(device))
        torch._fused_sgd_(
            device_params,
            device_grads,
            []
            if no_momentum_buffer
            else cast(list[Tensor], device_momentum_buffer_list),
            weight_decay=weight_decay,
            momentum=momentum,
            lr=lr,
            dampening=dampening,
            nesterov=nesterov,
            maximize=maximize,
            is_first_step=is_first_step,
            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,
            found_inf=device_found_inf,
        )

from typing import cast, Optional, Union

import torch
from torch import Tensor

from ._foreach import (
    _apply_foreach_maximize,
    _apply_foreach_weight_decay,
)
from .optimizer import (
    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,
    _device_dtype_check_for_fused,
    _differentiable_doc,
    _foreach_doc,
    _fused_doc,
    _maximize_doc,
    _params_doc,
    _to_scalar,
    _use_grad_for_differentiable,
    DeviceDict,
    Optimizer,
    ParamsT,

__all__ = ["SGD", "sgd"]


class SGD(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101
    def __init__(
        self,
        params: ParamsT,
        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,
        momentum: float = 0,
        dampening: float = 0,
        weight_decay: Union[float, Tensor] = 0,
        nesterov: bool = False,
        *,
        maximize: bool = False,
        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,
        differentiable: bool = False,
        fused: Optional[bool] = None,
    ):  # noqa: D107
        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:
            raise ValueError("Tensor lr must be 1-element")
        if lr < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid learning rate: {lr}")
        if momentum < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid momentum value: {momentum}")
        if weight_decay < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}")

        defaults = {
            "lr": lr,
            "momentum": momentum,
            "dampening": dampening,
            "weight_decay": weight_decay,
            "nesterov": nesterov,
            "maximize": maximize,
            "foreach": foreach,
            "differentiable": differentiable,
            "fused": fused,
        }
        if nesterov and (momentum <= 0 or dampening != 0):
            raise ValueError("Nesterov momentum requires a momentum and zero dampening")
        super().__init__(params, defaults)

        if fused:
            self._step_supports_amp_scaling = True
            self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = True
            if differentiable:
                raise RuntimeError("`fused` does not support `differentiable`")
            if foreach:
                raise RuntimeError("`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.")

    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105
        super().__setstate__(state)
        for group in self.param_groups:
            group.setdefault("nesterov", False)
            group.setdefault("maximize", False)
            group.setdefault("foreach", None)
            group.setdefault("differentiable", False)
            group.setdefault("fused", False)

    def _init_group(self, group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list):
        has_sparse_grad = False

        for p in group["params"]:
            if p.grad is not None:
                if group["fused"] and getattr(
                    self, "_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused", True
                ):
                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p)

                params.append(p)
                grads.append(p.grad)
                if p.grad.is_sparse:

                    state = self.state[p]
                    momentum_buffer_list.append(state.get("momentum_buffer"))

    @_use_grad_for_differentiable
    def step(self, closure=None):
        """Perform a single optimization step.

        Args:
            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model
                and returns the loss.

        loss = None
        if closure is not None:
            with torch.enable_grad():
                loss = closure()

        for group in self.param_groups:
            params: list[Tensor] = []
            grads: list[Tensor] = []
            momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]] = []

            has_sparse_grad = self._init_group(
                group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list
            )

            sgd(
                params,
                grads,
                momentum_buffer_list,
                weight_decay=group["weight_decay"],
                momentum=group["momentum"],
                lr=group["lr"],
                dampening=group["dampening"],
                nesterov=group["nesterov"],
                maximize=group["maximize"],
                has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,
                foreach=group["foreach"],
                fused=group["fused"],
                grad_scale=getattr(self, "grad_scale", None),
                found_inf=getattr(self, "found_inf", None),
            )

            if group["momentum"] != 0:
                # update momentum_buffers in state
                for p, momentum_buffer in zip(params, momentum_buffer_list):
                    state = self.state[p]
                    state["momentum_buffer"] = momentum_buffer

SGD.__doc__ = (
    r"""Implements stochastic gradient descent (optionally with momentum).

    .. math::
       \begin{aligned}
            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
            &\textbf{input}      : \gamma \text{ (lr)}, \: \theta_0 \text{ (params)}, \: f(\theta)
                \text{ (objective)}, \: \lambda \text{ (weight decay)},                          \\
            &\hspace{13mm} \:\mu \text{ (momentum)}, \:\tau \text{ (dampening)},
            \:\textit{ nesterov,}\:\textit{ maximize}                                     \\[-1.ex]
            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\
            &\textbf{for} \: t=1 \: \textbf{to} \: \ldots \: \textbf{do}                         \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{maximize}:                                       \\
            &\hspace{10mm}g_t           \leftarrow   -\nabla_{\theta} f_t (\theta_{t-1})         \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{else}                                                           \\
            &\hspace{10mm}g_t           \leftarrow   \nabla_{\theta} f_t (\theta_{t-1})          \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \lambda \neq 0                                           \\
            &\hspace{10mm} g_t \leftarrow g_t + \lambda  \theta_{t-1}                            \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\textbf{if} \: \mu \neq 0                                               \\
            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{if} \: t > 1                                                   \\
            &\hspace{15mm} \textbf{b}_t \leftarrow \mu \textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\tau) g_t           \\
            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{else}                                                          \\
            &\hspace{15mm} \textbf{b}_t \leftarrow g_t                                           \\
            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{if} \: \textit{nesterov}                                       \\
            &\hspace{15mm} g_t \leftarrow g_{t} + \mu \textbf{b}_t                               \\
            &\hspace{10mm}\textbf{else}                                                   \\[-1.ex]
            &\hspace{15mm} g_t  \leftarrow  \textbf{b}_t                                         \\
            &\hspace{5mm}\theta_t \leftarrow \theta_{t-1} - \gamma g_t                    \\[-1.ex]
            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\[-1.ex]
            &\bf{return} \:  \theta_t                                                     \\[-1.ex]
            &\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\[-1.ex]
       \end{aligned}

     Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from
     `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.
     """
    + rf"""
     Args:
         {_params_doc}
         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)
         momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)

         {_maximize_doc}
         {_foreach_doc}
         {_differentiable_doc}
         {_fused_doc}
     """
    + r"""

     Example:
         >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP
         >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)
         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()
         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()
         >>> optimizer.step()

     .. note::
         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from
         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.

         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as

         .. math::
             \begin{aligned}
                 v_{t+1} & = \mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\
                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \text{lr} * v_{t+1},
             \end{aligned}

         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\mu` denote the
         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.

         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and
         other frameworks which employ an update of the form

         .. math::
             \begin{aligned}
                 v_{t+1} & = \mu * v_{t} + \text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\
                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.
             \end{aligned}

         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.

         Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the
         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other
         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect
         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled
         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.

def sgd(
    params: list[Tensor],
    d_p_list: list[Tensor],
    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627
    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim
    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,
    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,
    fused: Optional[bool] = None,
    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,
    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,
    *,
    weight_decay: float,
    momentum: float,
    lr: float,
    dampening: float,
    nesterov: bool,
    maximize: bool,
):
    r"""Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.

    See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.
    """
    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change
    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach
    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl
    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.
    if foreach is None and fused is None:
        # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?
        # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting
        if not torch.jit.is_scripting():
            fused, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(
                params, differentiable=False, use_fused=False
            )
        else:
            foreach = False
            fused = False
    if foreach is None:
        foreach = False
    if fused is None:
        fused = False

    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():
        raise RuntimeError("torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers")
    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():
        raise RuntimeError("torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers")

    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():
        func = _multi_tensor_sgd
    elif fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():
        func = _fused_sgd
    else:
        func = _single_tensor_sgd

    func(
        params,
        d_p_list,
        momentum_buffer_list,
        weight_decay=weight_decay,
        momentum=momentum,
        lr=lr,
        dampening=dampening,
        nesterov=nesterov,
        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,
        maximize=maximize,
        grad_scale=grad_scale,
        found_inf=found_inf,
    )


def _single_tensor_sgd(
    params: list[Tensor],
    grads: list[Tensor],
    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
    *,
    weight_decay: float,
    momentum: float,
    lr: float,
    dampening: float,
    nesterov: bool,
    maximize: bool,
    has_sparse_grad: bool,
):
    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None

    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():
        lr = _to_scalar(lr)

    for i, param in enumerate(params):
        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]

        if weight_decay != 0:
            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules
            if isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):
                if weight_decay.requires_grad:
                    # usually this is the differentiable path, which is why the param.clone() is needed
                    grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)
                else:
                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)
            else:
                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)

        if momentum != 0:
            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]

            if buf is None:
                buf = grad.detach().clone()
                momentum_buffer_list[i] = buf
            else:
                buf.mul_(momentum).add_(grad, alpha=1 - dampening)

            if nesterov:
                grad = grad.add(buf, alpha=momentum)
            else:
                grad = buf

        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules
        if isinstance(lr, Tensor):
            if lr.requires_grad:
                param.addcmul_(grad, lr, value=-1)
            else:
                param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)
        else:
            param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)


def _multi_tensor_sgd(
    params: list[Tensor],
    grads: list[Tensor],
    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
    *,
    weight_decay: float,
    momentum: float,
    lr: float,
    dampening: float,
    nesterov: bool,
    maximize: bool,
    has_sparse_grad: bool,
):
    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None

    if len(params) == 0:
        return

    lr = _to_scalar(lr)

    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  # type: ignore[list-item]
        with_indices=True,
    )
    for (
        device_params_,
        device_grads_,
        device_momentum_buffer_list,
    ), indices in grouped_tensors.values():
        device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)
        device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)

        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(
            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads
        )

        # Use shared utility for maximize
        device_grads = _apply_foreach_maximize(device_grads, maximize)

        # Use shared utility for weight decay
        device_grads = _apply_foreach_weight_decay(
            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize
        )

        if momentum != 0:
            bufs: list[Tensor] = []

            all_states_with_momentum_buffer = True
            for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):
                if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:
                    all_states_with_momentum_buffer = False
                    break
                else:
                    bufs.append(cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i]))

            if all_states_with_momentum_buffer:
                torch._foreach_mul_(bufs, momentum)
                torch._foreach_add_(bufs, device_grads, alpha=1 - dampening)
            else:
                bufs = []
                for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):
                    if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:
                        buf = device_momentum_buffer_list[i] = momentum_buffer_list[
                            indices[i]
                        ] = device_grads[i].detach().clone()
                    else:
                        buf = cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i])
                        buf.mul_(momentum).add_(device_grads[i], alpha=1 - dampening)

                    bufs.append(buf)

            if nesterov:
                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, bufs, alpha=momentum)
            else:
                device_grads = bufs

        if not device_has_sparse_grad:
            # handle internal item() call if lr is a tensor
            if isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor) and torch.compiler.is_compiling():
                grads_x_lr = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, -lr)
                torch._foreach_add_(device_params, grads_x_lr)
            else:
                torch._foreach_add_(device_params, device_grads, alpha=-lr)
        else:
            # foreach APIs don't support sparse
            for i in range(len(device_params)):
                device_params[i].add_(device_grads[i], alpha=-lr)


def _fused_sgd(
    params: list[Tensor],
    grads: list[Tensor],
    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],
    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],
    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],
    *,
    weight_decay: float,
    momentum: float,
    lr: float,
    dampening: float,
    nesterov: bool,
    maximize: bool,
    has_sparse_grad: bool,
) -> None:
    if not params:
        return
    if has_sparse_grad:
        raise RuntimeError("`_fused_sgd` does not support sparse gradients")
    grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict = (
        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}
    )
    found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (
        {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}
    )

    no_momentum_buffer = momentum == 0
    is_first_step = (
        all(t is None for t in momentum_buffer_list) and not no_momentum_buffer
    )
    if is_first_step:
        for i, g in enumerate(grads):
            momentum_buffer_list[i] = torch.empty_like(g)
    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(
        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  # type: ignore[list-item]
        with_indices=False,
    )
    for (device, _), (
        (device_params_, device_grads_, device_momentum_buffer_list),
        _,
    ) in grouped_tensors.items():
        device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)
        device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)
        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None
        if grad_scale is not None:
            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict.setdefault(
                device, grad_scale.to(device)
            )
        if found_inf_dict is not None and found_inf is not None:
            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict.setdefault(device, found_inf.to(device))
        torch._fused_sgd_(
            device_params,
            device_grads,
            []
            if no_momentum_buffer
            else cast(list[Tensor], device_momentum_buffer_list),
            weight_decay=weight_decay,
            momentum=momentum,
            lr=lr,
            dampening=dampening,
            nesterov=nesterov,
            maximize=maximize,
            is_first_step=is_first_step,
            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,
            found_inf=device_found_inf,
        )
